name: Cameros and Iregua Valley Loop
name_original: Viguera, Santa María en Cameros, Cuevas Las Palomares – circular por Nalda
description: Tackle a difficult 30.6-mile mountain bike route through La Rioja, exploring ancient caves and diverse landscapes in the Sierra de Cameros.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Viguera is characterized by its diverse and dramatic landscapes within the Iregua River valley, at the foot of the Cameros Nuevos Mountains. The terrain includes significant elevations, with the Sierra de Moncalvillo to the northwest reaching over 1000 meters and the Sierra de Cameros Viejo to the east and south featuring peaks like Cerroyera (1405 m). This mountainous environment is shaped by canyons and river valleys, providing varied conditions for mountain bike trails. Best mountain bike trails around Viguera The most popular mountain bike trail is Cameros and Iregua Valley Loop, a 30.6 miles (49.2 km) trail that takes 4 hours 50 minutes to complete. This route offers extensive climbs through varied mountain and valley terrain. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Nalda Castle – Cañada Real Soriana Summit loop from La Rioja, a moderate 11.2 miles (18.0 km) path. This trail includes significant elevation gain, leading through the region's characteristic mountain landscapes. Local mountain bikers also love the Los Peñuejos Rock Formations – Senda del Fraile loop from La Rioja, a 18.8 miles (30.2 km) trail leading through mountainous areas with notable rock formations, often completed in about 3 hours 20 minutes. Mountain biking around Viguera is defined by the Iregua River valley, surrounding mountain ranges, and dramatic canyons. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Viguera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 50 reviews. More than 800 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Viguera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Viguera?
A: The terrain around Viguera is diverse, characterized by the Iregua River valley and the surrounding Cameros Nuevos Mountains. You'll encounter significant elevations, with areas like the Sierra de Moncalvillo reaching over 1000 meters. The landscape features dramatic canyons, river valleys, and some trails include technical sections with rock gardens, offering a varied and challenging experience.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of mountain bike trails in Viguera?
A: Viguera offers a wide range of mountain bike trails suitable for various skill levels. Out of 63 available routes, there are 3 easy trails, 30 moderate trails, and 30 difficult trails. This variety ensures options for both beginners and experienced riders seeking a challenge.

Q: Are there any notable natural features or landmarks to see while mountain biking near Viguera?
A: Yes, the region boasts several impressive natural features and historical landmarks. You can explore the dramatic canyons and river valleys, and during winter and spring, the 60-meter high Chorrón de Peña Puerta waterfall is particularly striking. Additionally, there are several caves to discover, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3157766" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuevas de Palomares&lt;/a&gt;, and various mountain peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4067711" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Moncalvillo&lt;/a&gt; offering panoramic views. Medieval bridges also add historical interest to some routes.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy the most about mountain biking in Viguera?
A: The mountain bike trails around Viguera are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 50 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, the dramatic scenery of the canyons and mountains, and the extensive network of trails that cater to different difficulty levels.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes available in Viguera?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes in Viguera are designed as loops. For example, the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993654724/cameros-and-iregua-valley-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cameros and Iregua Valley Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular option, covering nearly 50 km. Another great choice is the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38281443" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nalda Castle – Cañada Real Soriana Summit loop from La Rioja&lt;/a&gt;, which offers significant elevation gain through characteristic mountain landscapes.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Viguera?
A: The region is generally suitable for mountain biking for much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Winter can be impressive, especially for viewing the Chorrón de Peña Puerta waterfall at its fullest, but some higher elevation trails might be more challenging due to weather conditions. Summer can be warm, so early morning or late afternoon rides are often preferred.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Viguera?
A: While many trails in Viguera are moderate to difficult, there are 3 easy trails available that could be more suitable for families or those looking for a less strenuous ride. These routes typically feature less elevation gain and smoother surfaces, providing a more relaxed experience.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails in Viguera?
A: Many outdoor trails in Spain, especially multi-use paths, are dog-friendly, provided dogs are kept under control, often on a leash. While specific regulations for Viguera's mountain bike trails are not detailed, it's generally advisable to ensure your dog is well-behaved and to clean up after them. Always check local signage for any specific restrictions.

Q: Are there any less crowded mountain biking options in Viguera?
A: Viguera offers a network of 63 mountain bike trails, providing ample opportunities to find quieter routes. While popular trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993654724/cameros-and-iregua-valley-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cameros and Iregua Valley Loop&lt;/a&gt; attract many, exploring some of the less-known moderate or difficult trails, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20050951" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Path of the Muleteers loop from Sojuela&lt;/a&gt;, can offer a more secluded experience, especially during off-peak hours or seasons.

Q: How many mountain bike trails are there in Viguera?
A: There are 63 mountain bike trails available around Viguera, offering a wide variety of options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: Are there any cafes or places to eat near the mountain bike trails in Viguera?
A: Viguera is a municipality with local amenities. While specific cafes directly on the trails are unlikely, you can typically find places to eat and drink within the town of Viguera itself or in nearby villages, perfect for refueling before or after your ride.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike trails for advanced riders in Viguera?
A: For advanced riders seeking a challenge, Viguera offers 30 difficult trails. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31460557" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Peñuejos Rock Formations – Senda del Fraile loop from La Rioja&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 30 km route through mountainous areas with notable rock formations. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31456368" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moncalvillo Trail – Path of the Muleteers loop from Sojuela&lt;/a&gt;, which features over 1200 meters of elevation gain.
tips: A small ruined village. It is currently uninhabited although it was of great importance since it had factories of coarse cloth, yarn and cloths that supplied Soto. It is located on a hill between the ravines of Santa María and Hayedo at a height of a thousand meters.

Path from the top of Nalda to the road between Nalda and Viguera. Of high difficulty uphill due to the amount of loose stone and rock gardens. 180 ° curves that require a certain level of mastery of the MTB. But it pays off with beautiful views and great woodland passages. Better to do it accompanied by if the flies.
Quite an experience to visit these rock caves, very very curious
Incredible place! Reddish stone formations among vineyards.

faqs.question: What makes this mountain bike route difficult?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as a difficult mountain bike ride due to its significant distance of nearly 50 km and an elevation gain of approximately 1,375 meters. You can expect challenging ascents and technical descents, especially given the presence of enduro mountain bike descents in the Nalda area. It's best suited for experienced mountain bikers.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking for the entire circular route isn't detailed, Nalda is a key point on the route and a good starting option. For the Cuevas de Palomares section, parking is available in Nalda, from where the caves are accessible. Viguera also serves as a potential starting or access point.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers a mix of natural beauty and historical sites. You'll pass by the Ruins of Santa María en Cameros, the impressive Cuevas de Palomares near Nalda, and the unique Los Peñuejos Rock Formations. The Cerro 1800 Trail is also part of the experience.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain and scenery will I encounter?
faqs.answer: You'll experience diverse landscapes across La Rioja. Expect forested areas, particularly around Santa María en Cameros with its beech forests, rocky terrain near the Cuevas de Palomares, and potentially scenic descents near Viguera, which is associated with a Roman road trail. The Nalda area also features terrain designed for demanding mountain biking.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to ride this trail is generally during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder and the natural scenery is at its most vibrant. Summer can be very hot in La Rioja, making the long distance and elevation gain more challenging. Winter might bring colder temperatures and potential snow at higher elevations in the Sierra de Cameros.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this route or within Sierra de Cameros?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access this mountain bike route or to ride within the Sierra de Cameros natural area. However, always respect local regulations and private property signs. The Cuevas de Palomares are freely accessible, but check for any specific local guidelines if you plan to explore them extensively.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed on many trails in the region, it's always best to keep them on a leash, especially in natural areas like the Sierra de Cameros, to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Given the route's difficulty and length, ensure your dog is fit enough for such a demanding ride.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other mountain bike trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this circular route incorporates sections of several other trails. You'll ride parts of the Cno de Nalda-Trevijano, Senda de Nalda a Luezas, Via Romana del Iregua, Camino de Torrecilla a San Román, and various segments of the Nalda Bike Park, including Bike Park Rojo. Other smaller paths like Senda de los Pescadores and Senda del Monolito are also integrated.

faqs.question: Are there amenities like cafes or accommodation near the trail?
faqs.answer: The towns of Viguera and Nalda, which are part of this circular route, offer basic amenities. Nalda, in particular, is a good spot to find cafes, restaurants, and potentially accommodation options, especially after a long ride. Santa María en Cameros is a smaller village, so amenities there might be more limited.

faqs.question: What essential gear should I bring for this difficult mountain bike ride?
faqs.answer: For this difficult and long mountain bike ride, you should bring a well-maintained mountain bike suitable for challenging terrain, a helmet, appropriate cycling apparel, plenty of water and snacks, a repair kit (including spare tubes/patch kit), a first-aid kit, and navigation tools (like a GPS device or the komoot app with the route downloaded). Given the varied terrain, consider knee and elbow pads for technical sections.

faqs.question: Is public transport an option to reach the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Public transport options directly to the specific trailheads for this circular route might be limited, especially for carrying a mountain bike. It's generally more convenient to access the route by car, particularly to Nalda or Viguera. Check local bus schedules for connections to these towns from larger hubs like Logroño, but be aware of bike transport policies.

waypoints.name: Ruins of Santa María en Cameros
